title: carex × limula
intro: Carex × limula, also known as the variegated sedge, is a hybrid plant species in the sedge family, derived from the cross between Carex limula and Carex montana. The plant's roots and rhizomes have been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asia, to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Carex × limula are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being. Carex × limula is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal benefits and support health.
